title: Order Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Order intake, stock checks, and ERP entry are now scripted enough for AI order-management systems to handle end-to-end for standard orders.
rationales.toolMaturity: E-commerce and ERP order-automation tools are mature and near-universal in retail and manufacturing order pipelines.
rationales.adoption: Automated order processing is a mainstream expectation across retail, manufacturing, and distribution.
rationales.agenticExposure: Order management is explicitly the kind of digital pipeline an autonomous agent can traverse end-to-end for the majority of standard, non-custom orders.

tasksAtRisk: Processing incoming purchase and sales orders
Checking stock availability and confirming quantities
Entering orders into ERP or order-management systems
Sending order confirmations and basic status updates
tasksGrowing: Resolving order discrepancies and customer disputes
Managing complex or custom order configurations
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4151 (Order Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI order-management systems now take standard orders from intake to confirmation without a human, which is why this role sits at High risk. Clerks handling discrepancies and custom configurations hold the more defensible ground.
